purpose: Detector-level AUC-DEGRADATION harness: for a labeled corpus, measure how much each detector's P(machine > human) separation degrades when the machine windows are paraphrased (human windows are the fixed reference class, never attacked). The corpus-level / population-ranking counterpart of paraphrase_ladder's per-(signal x rung) single-text decay curve — the two measure at different levels and do not overlap. M1 is model-free orchestration over INJECTED detector scores (CI-runnable, no model imported at load); the GPU re-scoring over binoculars_audit / surprisal_backend / fast_detect_curvature / variance_audit is the M2 seam. Per (detector x rung) it emits the discriminative AUC (WMW-U via validation_harness.fallback_roc_auc, ORIENTED by each detector's known machine-vs-human sign so a "machine lower" signal is not read as inverted), TPR at FPR budgets {0.05, 0.10}, and the delta from the unattacked rung 0. The sign/direction is pinned in a test (the silent-inversion guard). NOT a detector and NOT a verdict: emits NO aggregate robustness or accuracy scalar (no robustness_score / auc_retained / area_under_decay / is_robust / overall_robustness), sets no threshold, feeds no calibration or selector. The bundled M1 paraphraser is a deterministic stdlib lexical proxy (proxy_stdlib), honestly weaker than a neural paraphraser — a flat curve under it is a LOWER bound on degradation, never robustness. The ClaimLicense refuses any "robust to paraphrase" claim, quoting Sadasivan's separability ceiling (arXiv:2303.11156). calibration_status heuristic.
use_when: You have a labeled machine/human corpus and a paraphrase attack, and want the per-detector AUC degradation curve and the FPR/TPR operating-point shift across attack rungs
You want the honest-FPR read (TPR at FPR=0.05 and 0.10 before and after attack), not AUC alone
You are stress-testing the structural-detection thesis against the Sadasivan TV bound
do_not_use_when: You want a single robustness or accuracy number — this surface refuses any aggregate score
You want a provenance verdict for a document — it is not a detector
You want proof a detector is paraphrase-robust — a flat stdlib-proxy curve is never that
You want a per-(signal x rung) single-text decay curve — that is paraphrase_ladder, a different measurement level

inputs.target: an injected-scores JSON (detectors, n_rungs, machine_texts, human_texts, and per-(detector, rung) machine/human score lists); the M2 GPU operator supplies real detector scores instead

compute.cost_note: M1 is stdlib + the pure-Python WMW-U AUC helper; CPU only, no model loads, no API costs (detector re-scoring is the GPU-gated M2 seam)

examples.description: Run the harness over an injected-scores JSON (M1, model-free)

references: Sadasivan et al. 2023, Can AI-Generated Text Be Reliably Detected?; arXiv:2303.11156: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11156
Krishna et al. 2023, Paraphrasing Evades Detectors (DIPPER); arXiv:2303.13408: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.13408
Hans et al. 2024, Binoculars; arXiv:2401.12070: https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.12070
